leagueBriefing: Jon Rahm ended a lengthy individual drought with a clinical closing 64 at LIV Golf Hong Kong, while Dustin Johnson's retooled 4Aces finally broke through for their first team win in nearly three years.

games.headline: Rahm closes with 64 to win Hong Kong as 4Aces snap team title drought
games.brief: Jon Rahm returned to the winner's circle at HSBC LIV Golf Hong Kong with a composed final-round 6-under 64, finishing at 23-under to beat Thomas Detry by three shots and claim his first individual win in 539 days. Rahm seized control down the stretch with a crucial birdie at the 14th, then separated as Thomas Pieters faltered, turning a tight race into a comfortable closing margin. In the team race, Dustin Johnson's 4Aces GC posted a dominant final-day 16-under aggregate and won by six, ending a 974-day wait for a team victory and validating their rebuilt lineup featuring Detry and Anthony Kim.
bullets: Rahm won the individual title at 23-under (66-62-65-64), three clear of runner-up Thomas Detry at 20-under.
Thomas Pieters finished solo third at 19-under, his first LIV individual podium.
4Aces GC won the team title at 58-under, six ahead of Smash GC (52-under).
Dustin Johnson led the 4Aces on Sunday with a 5-under 65; Kim and Pieters shot 66, Detry shot 67.
Rahm called the win 'very relieving' after consecutive runner-up finishes to start the season.
games.context: The Hong Kong result tightens the early season race and reinforces both Rahm's individual consistency and 4Aces' renewed status as a team contender.
